channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Review by Senate Democrats finds more unreported luxury trips by Clarence Thomas
item.description: A report by Democrats on the Judiciary Committee found additional travel taken in 2021 by Thomas but not reported on his annual financial disclosure, including trips on private jets and a yacht trip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Joe Manchin calls Democratic Party ‘toxic,’ blames progressives
item.description: Sen. Joe Manchin, I-W.V., blamed progressives for turning the Democratic Party "toxic" as he prepares to retire from the Senate at the end of this term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ocasio-Cortez faces uncertain political future
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.) is facing an uncertain future after a pivotal setback this week, when she lost her race to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee. The 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly’s (D-Va.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Trudo, NewsNation

item.title: Lara Trump says she's removing herself from consideration to be Florida senator
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Lara Trump, the daughter-in-law of President-elect Donald Trump, said Saturday that she's removing herself from consideration to be a Florida senator — ending speculation that she could replace Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lara Trump removes herself from consideration for Marco Rubio’s US Senate seat
item.description: Lara Trump, former RNC co-chair and President-elect Trump's daughter-in-law, said Saturday she is withdrawing from consideration to replace Marco Rubio in the U.S. Senate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump nominates TV producer, creator of 'The Apprentice,' to serve in new administration
item.description: President-elect Trump has tapped Mark Burnett, a TV producer and the creator of "The Apprentice," to serve in a diplomatic role in the United Kingdom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What Trump's decision to wade into spending fight tells us about the next 4 years
item.description: After days of threats and demands, Donald Trump had little to show for it once lawmakers passed a budget deal.
item.dc:creator: Chris Megerian, Stephen Groves, Jill Colvin And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers react to stopgap funding and averting government shutdown
item.description: Lawmakers are having their say after President Biden signed a stopgap bill into law on Saturday, extending government funding into March and avoiding a shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Funding saga forecasts future struggles for Speaker Johnson
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) averted a government shutdown, but the days-long rollercoaster as the leaders cycled through multiple plans leaves serious questions about how the Speaker will manage the House GOP under President-elect Trump — and whether he can keep his gavel.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fani Willis disqualification may doom Trump’s Georgia case
item.description: Fulton County District Attorney Fani Willis’s (D) disqualification from Georgia's election interference case against President-elect Trump and his allies could mark the beginning of the end of the prosecution.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Senate review of Supreme Court ethics finds more luxury trips and urges enforceable code of conduct
item.description: A nearly two-year investigation by Democratic senators of Supreme Court ethics details more luxury travel by Justice Clarence Thomas and urges Congress to establish a way to enforce a new code of conduct.
item.dc:creator: Lindsay Whitehurst, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Were undercover sources from other DOJ agencies present on Jan. 6? Grassley, Johnson demand answers
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: Senate Republicans demand answers on whether confidential human sources from DOJ agencies beyond the FBI were used on Jan. 6, 2021, while also questioning IG Horowitz's review of records.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate sends spending deal to Biden's desk, averting shutdown
item.description: The Senate in the early hours of Saturday passed a stopgap funding package, avoiding a government shutdown that would have furloughed hundreds of thousands of federal workers and bringing a tumultuous week in Congress to a close.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate passes bill to boost Social Security benefits for some
item.description: The Senate voted overwhelmingly in the early hours of Saturday to pass legislation to boost Social Security benefits for more than 2 million Americans.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What is next for Florida's insurance market as CFO Patronis steps down
item.description: He is the state's top insurance official, but Florida's Chief Financial Officer Jimmy Patronis is stepping down in a few months to run for Congress. Governor Ron DeSantis will appoint a temporary replacement to the CFO position, as Florida residents continue to face a fragile insurance market.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms 235th Biden judge, surpassing Trump’s record
item.description: Senate Majority Leader Chuck Schumer (D-N.Y.) marked the achievement after the Senate confirmed Serena Raquel Murillo to serve as district judge for the Central District of California.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Texas coal-fired power plant to switch to clean energy after receiving more than $1 billion in federal money
item.description: San Miguel Electric Cooperative's plan to turn into a solar and battery plant will leave only 14 coal-fired power plants in the state.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Juan Salinas Ii, KPRC 2

item.title: A judge says Missouri's abortion ban isn’t enforceable, but there's no start date for abortions
item.description: JEFFERSON CITY, Mo. (AP) — A judge ruled Friday that Missouri’s near-total abortion ban is unenforceable under a new constitutional amendment, though Planned Parenthood said the decision is still not sufficient for it to resume providing abortions in the state.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Adams says Dems 'missed the memo' on migrant crisis and it hurt the party
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams sat down with PIX on Politics host Dan Mannarino for an exclusive year-end interview, looking back at what was undoubtedly a very eventful 2024 and ahead to what’s in store for 2025.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Deal spares federal workers before Christmas but there's concern about possible job cuts under Trump
item.description: Johnny Zuagar says he tried to hide his worries about a potential government shutdown from his three boys as he weighs how much to spend on Christmas presents.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden signs bill to avert government shutdown
item.description: After weeks of back and forth, the House and Senate have agreed on a spending deal to avoid a government shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: House passes bill to avert government shutdown after whirlwind funding fight
item.description: The House approved legislation to avert a government shutdown hours before the deadline Friday, sending the bill to the Senate for consideration after a whirlwind week on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota House tie in peril after judge rules DFL lawmaker can’t be sworn in
item.description: A district court judge on Friday granted a challenge to Democratic Rep.-elect Curtis Johnson’s residency, blocking him from taking office.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson and Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Government funding bill clears Congress and heads to President Biden, averting a shutdown
item.description: The Senate has rushed through final passage of a bipartisan plan to fund federal operations and prevent a government shutdown. It now goes to President Joe Biden, who is expected to quickly sign it into law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump’s reelection victory pushes some LGTBQ Minneapolis residents to safety training
item.description: For some marginalized communities the second Trump term is fraught with fears over personal safety. In Minneapolis, one organization is helping with de-escalation services and gun training.
item.dc:creator: Odette Yousef, MPR News

item.title: Republicans scramble for funding plan C as shutdown deadline draws near
item.description: Congress is racing toward a shutdown at the end of the day Friday, and Republicans appear no closer to finding a path forward that will keep the lights on and appease President-elect Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Chip Roy: Trump wants Social Security benefits intact
item.description: Chip Roy believes Trump doesn't actually want to cut from Social Security and other essential benefits on which Americans rely.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Dems say 'hell no' to GOP plan B on government funding, debt ceiling
item.description: House Democrats are rallying in overwhelming opposition to the Republicans' second stab at a government funding bill, vowing to sink it on the House floor and force GOP leaders back to the table to negotiate a bipartisan compromise.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Trump backs plan B spending deal, urges swift passage
item.description: “All Republicans, and even the Democrats, should do what is best for our Country, and vote ‘YES’ for this Bill, TONIGHT!“ Trump posted on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: How much power would RFK Jr. have at HHS? A former health secretary weighs in
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., is making the case with senators that he should lead Health and Human Services. Kathleen Sebelius, who had the job under Obama, explains the power and limits of the role.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gorenstein, MPR News

item.title: Government funding plan collapses as Trump makes new demands
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has abruptly rejected a bipartisan plan to prevent a Christmastime government shutdown, instead telling House Speaker Mike Johnson and Republicans to essentially renegotiate — days before a deadline when federal funding runs out.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump, Vance call for clean CR, debt ceiling hike, urge GOP to sink Johnson compromise
item.description: President-elect Trump and Vice President-elect Vance on Wednesday called for Republicans to approve a clean stopgap funding bill paired with a hike to the debt ceiling, saying the previous government-funding measure negotiated by Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) should be torpedoed.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: The president of the NCAA calls for a ban on 'prop bets' in college sports
item.description: As sports betting has spread across the U.S., college athletes have increasingly faced harassment from bettors. A federal ban on wagers that concern individual performances could help, the NCAA says.
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota-backed bill to make bald eagles the national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: The bald eagle could soon become the national bird of the United States after a bill backed by Minnesota legislators passed the U.S. House and Senate.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster
item.description: “Selzer’s polling ‘miss’ was not an astonishing coincidence — it was intentional,” the complaint states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, Fox 5

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Report documents family separations during 1st Trump term
item.description: A report examining years of family separation policies enacted under the Trump administration is being released Monday by several migrant advocacy nonprofit organizations to remind the public and lawmakers of what transpired, Border Report has learned.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5
